Name: Zuni Café (8.8)
Blurb: SF institution since 1979 — wood-fired roast chicken for two with warm bread salad, oysters, and espresso granita.

Name: State Bird Provisions (9.0)
Blurb: Michelin-starred Californian small plates served dim-sum style from roving carts. The namesake fried quail is mandatory.

Name: Rich Table (9.2)
Blurb: Playful Michelin-starred Cal cuisine — sardine chips and porcini doughnuts have been famous for a decade for a reason.

Name: Lazy Bear (9.4)
Blurb: Two-Michelin-star communal dinner party in the Mission. Tasting menu, hearth cooking, and a genuinely fun room.

Name: Californios (9.4)
Blurb: Two Michelin stars for the most refined modern Mexican tasting menu in the country. Caviar-topped masa everything.

Name: Benu (9.2)
Blurb: Corey Lee's three-Michelin-star Asian-inflected tasting menu. The faux-shark-fin soup is an all-timer splurge.

Name: Atelier Crenn (9.2)
Blurb: Dominique Crenn's three-star 'poetic culinaria' — the menu is literally a poem, seafood-and-vegetable driven.

Name: Saison (9.0)
Blurb: Open-hearth fine dining where nearly everything touches live fire. Legendary caviar and antelope courses.

Name: House of Prime Rib (9.2)
Address: 1906 Van Ness Ave, San Francisco, CA 94109
Blurb: Old-school SF at its best — prime rib carved tableside from silver carts, spinning salads, and stiff martinis.

Name: Swan Oyster Depot (9.4)
Blurb: Century-old marble counter with 18 stools. Crab Louie, smoked salmon 'Sicilian sashimi', and the best line in SF.

Name: Tartine Bakery (9.0)
Blurb: The bakery that changed American bread. Morning buns, country loaf, and banana cream tarts worth the Mission queue.

Name: La Taqueria (9.0)
Blurb: James Beard-winning Mission burrito — no rice, griddled 'dorado style' carnitas. A pilgrimage site.

Name: Nopa (9.0)
Blurb: Wood-fired urban rustic Californian open late. Pork chop, flatbread, and the burger that anchors the whole city.

Name: Flour + Water (9.0)
Blurb: The Mission's handmade-pasta temple. Do the pasta tasting menu and don't look back.

Name: Kokkari Estiatorio (9.4)
Blurb: Sumptuous Greek taverna with a roaring fireplace — grilled lamb chops, whole fish, and avgolemono soup.

Name: Gary Danko (9.4)
Address: 800 N Point St, San Francisco, CA 94109
Blurb: Classic tasting-menu fine dining near the wharf. Impeccable service, roasted lobster, and a rolling cheese cart.

Name: Acquerello (9.4)
Blurb: Two-Michelin-star refined Italian in a former chapel. Parmesan budino and a legendary Italian wine list.

Name: Mister Jiu's (8.8)
Blurb: Michelin-starred Cantonese in a historic Chinatown banquet hall — quail with liver mousse, Dutch crunch BBQ pork buns.

Name: Che Fico (8.8)
Blurb: Buzzy Cal-Italian with Roman-style pizza, house salumi, and a room that always feels like a party.

Name: Burma Superstar (9.0)
Blurb: The Richmond's Burmese star — fermented tea leaf salad tossed tableside and coconut chicken noodles.

Name: San Ho Won (9.0)
Blurb: Corey Lee's Michelin-starred Korean charcoal BBQ — galbi cooked over binchotan, perfect banchan.

Name: Cotogna (9.2)
Blurb: Rustic Italian next door to Quince — wood-fired meats, ethereal agnolotti, and one of the city's best-value prix fixes.

Name: Sons & Daughters (9.2)
Blurb: Michelin-starred Nob Hill tasting menu, Nordic-leaning and hyper-seasonal — named to North America's 50 Best.

Name: Nari (9.0)
Blurb: Pim Techamuanvivit's Michelin-starred Thai in Japantown — refined curries and khao soi that punch way above.

Name: Osito (9.4)
Blurb: Michelin-starred live-fire tasting menu — every course cooked over open flame at one long communal counter.

Name: Hog Island Oyster Co. (9.2)
Blurb: Tomales Bay oysters shucked to order with a Bay Bridge view. The grilled cheese and clam chowder are sleepers.

Name: Liholiho Yacht Club (9.0)
Blurb: Heritage Hawaiian-Californian — tuna poke on nori crackers, squid-ink rice, and the baked Hawaii dessert.

Name: Kin Khao (8.8)
Address: 55 Cyril Magnin St, San Francisco, CA 94102
Blurb: Michelin-starred Thai hidden in a Union Square hotel — rabbit green curry and pretty-hot wings, zero compromises.

Name: Zazie (9.2)
Blurb: Cole Valley's beloved brunch garden — gingerbread pancakes, tip-free since 2015, and worth every minute of the wait.

Name: Angler (8.8)
Blurb: Saison's wood-fired seafood sibling on the water — radicchio 'XO' salad, whole fish, and Parker House rolls.
